executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 Canadian organizations publicly listed as victims by 21 different ransomware groups in the last 48 hours. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 21 victims, followed closely by the CLOP gang with 13 victims and the Play ransomware operation with 12 victims. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (consumer brand), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and MRC de Maskinongé municipal district. This represents a coordinated mass extortion campaign across critical sectors including healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and manufacturing.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra law firm, Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, and Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a severe ransomware crisis with 103 organizations publicly victimized across all provinces in a 48-hour period. Critical infrastructure targets include public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Bais Yaakov), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in the feed for this period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major consumer brand), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail chain), and Kids & Company (childcare services) were compromised. No incidents identified at Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, or Shoppers Drug Mart.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loans/financial services) suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers and Trucash (financial services) were also compromised. No incidents identified at major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO).

report_2w.executive_summary: URGENT: A critical Linux vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431) granting root access is being actively exploited in the wild and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. CCCS issued an advisory on May 1st. Additionally, a critical cPanel/WHM authentication bypass (CVE-2026-41940) is under mass exploitation for ransomware deployment. CCCS also warned on April 30th about cyber criminals using social engineering to compromise enterprise cloud software environments. Multiple ransomware groups (Everest, ShinyHunters, Stormous, M3RX) have posted new victims, including Canadian financial services provider Symcor targeted by Everest ransomware.

items.description: Linux root access vulnerability actively exploited in the wild
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: CCCS issued three critical advisories in the past two weeks: CVE-2026-31431 (Linux root access, actively exploited), CVE-2026-41940 (cPanel authentication bypass under mass exploitation), and a warning about social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments. The Everest ransomware group claims to have breached Symcor, a major Canadian financial data processing company serving banks and credit unions.
canada_landscape.financial: Symcor (Canadian financial data processing provider) targeted by Everest ransomware group
